# Ordered Oils fix queue as of 2026-04-03.
# Source of truth: current `tests/fixtures/oils/pass-list.txt` plus `tests/oils_spec.rs`
# skip rules and `tests/common/oils_format.rs` case parsing.
# Current totals: 2024 pass / 435 xfail / 79 skip, with 42 whole-file skips in `tests/oils_spec.rs`.
#
# ── How the Oils test infra works ──
# Runner:      tests/oils_spec.rs (datatest_stable harness, discovers .test.sh files)
# Format:      tests/common/oils_format.rs parses the Oils .test.sh format into cases
# Pass-list:   tests/fixtures/oils/pass-list.txt — one "file:case name" per line.
#              Everything IN the list must pass; everything NOT in it defaults to xfail.
#              Unexpected passes and regressions are both hard failures.
# Regenerate:  OILS_GENERATE_PASS_LIST=1 cargo test --test oils_spec 2>/dev/null \
#                | grep '^PASS_LIST:' | sed 's/^PASS_LIST://' | sort > pass-list.txt
#              ⚠ Run multi-threaded (default); single-threaded gives fewer passes due to
#              pre-existing test-isolation flakiness. Take the union of 3 runs for stability.
# Run one file: cargo test --test oils_spec -- "assign-extended" --nocapture --test-threads=1
# Verbose xfails: OILS_VERBOSE_XFAIL=1 cargo test --test oils_spec -- "file" --nocapture
# Skip list:   skip_files() in oils_spec.rs — whole-file skips for non-applicable tests.
# Override precedence in .test.sh format: N-I > BUG > OK > default (per-shell).
#
# ── Backlog ──
# This is a file-level backlog, ordered by recommended implementation sequence.
# Each line is one Oils `.test.sh` file with remaining xfails.
# Permanent whole-file skips are intentionally excluded.
# `nul-bytes.test` and `unicode.test` stay deferred to Milestone 9 work.
# `builtin-cd.test` and `redirect.test` stay on the queue, but expect a small VFS/symlink/fd tail to remain until M9.

## Parser, Expansion, Quoting, Assignment
- sh-func.test (xfail=2) shell-function parsing / invocation tail
- word-eval.test (xfail=1) word evaluation corner cases
- temp-binding.test (xfail=1) temporary binding semantics
- if_.test (xfail=1) `if` control-flow edge case
- paren-ambiguity.test (xfail=1) parenthesis parse ambiguity tail
- smoke.test (xfail=1) keep green after upstream parser fixes
- subshell.test (xfail=1) small subshell semantic tail tied to core parsing/execution

## Builtins, Shell State, Options, Control, Tracing
- builtin-read.test (xfail=26) `read` / `mapfile` semantics remain the biggest builtin gap
- builtin-printf.test (xfail=14) format handling, escapes, integer conversion, `%T`, `%b`, `-v`
- sh-options.test (xfail=10) shell option state, invocation flags, `$-`, `shopt`
- builtin-type-bash.test (xfail=14) `type` / PATH / alias / function precedence
- dbracket.test (xfail=6) `[[ ... ]]` parse/runtime semantics
- xtrace.test (xfail=5) tracing output fidelity and `PS4` behavior
- vars-special.test (xfail=12) `BASHPID`, `LINENO`, `$_`, startup defaults, pipeline state
- builtin-set.test (xfail=7) `set` flag handling and side effects
- errexit.test (xfail=12) `set -e` / redirection / alias / function behavior
- builtin-echo.test (xfail=3) escape behavior and bash echo quirks
- command_.test (xfail=11) command lookup / PATH / cache / script execution behavior
- builtin-meta.test (xfail=10) `command -v/-V`, `builtin`, `command -p`
- builtin-eval-source.test (xfail=7) `eval` / `source` option and PATH semantics
- builtin-vars.test (xfail=7) `unset`, `local`, export / scope edge cases
- builtin-bracket.test (xfail=10) `test` / `[` predicate fidelity
- builtin-special.test (xfail=8) POSIX special builtin semantics
- strict-options.test (xfail=8) strict mode control-flow / argv behavior
- builtin-getopts.test (xfail=6) `getopts`, `OPTIND`, `OPTARG` edge cases
- loop.test (xfail=6) `break` / `continue` / loop validation tail
- sh-options-bash.test (xfail=4) `SHELLOPTS` / `BASHOPTS` fidelity
- builtin-meta-assign.test (xfail=1) assignment-builtin interaction tail
- case_.test (xfail=3) `case` parsing / pattern tail
- exit-status.test (xfail=3) shell exit-code bookkeeping
- builtin-type.test (xfail=2) smaller `type` output / keyword cases
- builtin-bash.test (xfail=3) `bash` / `sh` builtin tail
- builtin-misc.test (xfail=2) leftover builtin behavior gaps
- builtin-dirs.test (xfail=1) directory-stack builtin tail
- vars-bash.test (xfail=1) bash-specific variable tail
## Arrays, Indirection, Regex, Introspection
- array-sparse.test (xfail=12) sparse-array semantics and negative-index handling
- var-ref.test (xfail=15) indirect expansion and guarded expansion semantics
- array.test (xfail=10) array assignment / env-binding / strict-array behavior
- nameref.test (xfail=13) `declare -n` and reference semantics
- regex.test (xfail=7) `[[ ... =~ ... ]]` parse/runtime regex behavior
- array-assign.test (xfail=10) assignment forms for arrays
- array-assoc.test (xfail=5) associative-array tail
- introspect.test (xfail=7) introspection / serialization tail
- array-compat.test (xfail=2) compatibility behavior for array corner cases

## Execution, Redirects, Globs, Filesystem Fidelity
- sh-usage.test (xfail=8) `sh` / `bash` invocation and argument handling
- bugs.test (xfail=7) mixed regression bucket; sweep after core semantics are stable
- redirect.test (xfail=7) redirection semantics; leave the final fd/VFS tail for M9
- builtin-cd.test (xfail=7) `cd` / `pwd` semantics; leave the final symlink/PWD fidelity tail for M9
- redirect-multi.test (xfail=1) multi-redirect word expansion and ambiguity tail
- glob.test (xfail=5) remaining glob semantics and locale/order edge cases
- tilde.test (xfail=3) tilde expansion tail
- globignore.test (xfail=4) `GLOBIGNORE` interaction semantics
- process-sub.test (xfail=3) process substitution semantics
- serialize.test (xfail=5) shell state serialization / `%q` / `set` output fidelity
- redirect-command.test (xfail=5) command + redirect interaction semantics
- redir-order.test (xfail=4) redirect ordering semantics
- extglob-files.test (xfail=3) extglob filesystem matching tail
- extglob-match.test (xfail=2) extglob matcher semantics
- pipeline.test (xfail=9) pipeline execution semantics
- glob-bash.test (xfail=1) bash-specific glob behavior tail

## Deferred Until Milestone 9
- nul-bytes.test (xfail=16) binary/NUL semantics; maps to M9 binary-encoding work
- unicode.test (xfail=7) Unicode escape / invalid-codepoint fidelity; keep with M9 binary/text fidelity work

## Parser-Adjacent Backlog
- alias.test (xfail=26) keep at the end; current blocker is moving alias expansion earlier than the post-AST `split_whitespace()` hack in `src/interpreter/walker.rs`, likely between tokenization and parsing in `src/interpreter/mod.rs` and/or by changing `RustBash::exec()` parse granularity; this is not proven to require `brush_parser` changes
- parse-errors.test (xfail=6) remaining: long token (needs python2), `${%}` parser literal, `}` on second line, interactive parse error, array literal inside array
- command-sub.test (xfail=15) command-sub quoting and newline semantics; exit-code propagation fixed
